title: Gate evidence bundle (v0.2)
description: One readable JSON document binding per-stage in-toto-shaped Statement entries to a single digest-addressed subject (the PR head commit). Contract v0.2 — v0 (LOCKED — resolution of TzolkinB/skills#102) plus an additive `EMPTY` execution result (TzolkinB/skills#111, v0.1, ADR-0031) plus the internal witness:// -> gate:// identifier rename (TzolkinB/skills#113, v0.2, ADR-0033) — a rename only, no data-model change. Backward-compatible with v0.1 in shape (only identifiers moved); the schemaVersion increment is the honest signal the identifiers changed, so an old producer emitting the old const is correctly rejected rather than silently accepted. Execution evidence = Playwright JSON and/or a Cypress Module API result (added additively per contract Q1: `stage` is a free string, so a new framework needs no schema change or version bump); no numeric confidence. audit-test rides either OPAQUE (Markdown inline, caps at canary) or PARSED (verdict.result+label — the B→A graduation, TzolkinB/skills#49; PASSED+proven unlocks ship). Extensibility = versioned predicateType URIs + ignore-unrecognized + per-entry annotations.

subject.description: One shared subject at the bundle top level (contract Q4). Per-entry subject is reserved for future cross-run aggregation.

producer.description: SLSA builder/metadata — who produced this evidence and when.

statement.description: An in-toto Statement carrying one stage's evidence (kept readable — no DSSE/base64 in v0).

evidencePredicate.description: A stage's evidence. `stage` is a data field (contract Q1), so new stages need no schema change.

verdict.comment: verdict.confidence is CUT in v0 (contract Q6). Its arrival is the signal Gate has earned a number — it requires a schemaVersion bump.

gatePredicate.description: The derived gate decision (gate-function spec v0, LOCKED — #103). Honesty guard #3: additionalProperties:false and NO numeric field anywhere — a validator rejects a smuggled number, so re-adding confidence forces a schemaVersion bump.
